topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, a small, sharp intrusion in the otherwise quiet afternoon. Liam stared at his phone, his thumb hovering over the screen. It was a post from Ms. Evans, a candid photo taken at a recent school event, captioned with a thoughtful reflection on pedagogy. A knot tightened in his chest, a familiar, unwelcome clenching. He scrolled through her feed, noticing the likes—hundreds of them. He felt a sudden, hot flush creep up his neck, his jaw clenching involuntarily. It wasn't just seeing her; it was seeing her so openly, so connected to the wider world, while he remained stuck in the confines of the classroom, watching her from a distance. He quickly minimized the app, the screen suddenly too bright, and tried to focus on the textbook, but the words swam before his eyes.

[story 2]

Maya felt a strange prickle of unease as she unlocked her social media profile. There, in the feed, was a picture of Mr. Harrison laughing, a picture she hadn't seen in months, shared with a caption about a recent professional development seminar. A tightness settled in Maya’s stomach, a churning sensation she couldn't quite settle. She watched the comments flood in—compliments, congratulations—and a sour taste bloomed on her tongue. She picked up her pen, the cheap plastic feeling slick in her hand, and began aggressively underlining passages in her notes, her movements jerky. She needed to feel something tangible, something that wasn't this intangible, simmering feeling of being overlooked, of being outside the circle she desperately wanted to be a part of.

[story 3]

When Chloe opened Instagram, the image of her history teacher appeared instantly. It was a snapshot from a weekend trip, clearly happy and relaxed. Chloe felt a distinct shift in her posture; her shoulders slumped slightly as she absorbed the visual evidence. A sharp, almost painful awareness sparked behind her eyes. She watched the interaction—the little heart reactions, the shared moments—and a deep dissatisfaction settled over her. She rapidly swiped through the posts, her breathing becoming shallow and quick. She slammed her phone face-down on the desk, the sound echoing too loudly in the library quiet, and forced herself to reread the assigned reading, trying to drown out the insistent, throbbing sense of inadequacy rising within her.
